2606.31800.title: Evo-PI: Aligning Medical Reasoning via Evolving Principle-Guided Supervision
2606.31800.paper_summary: Despite recent progress, the reasoning capabilities of large multimodal language models (MLLMs) remain fundamentally constrained by static supervision, where fixed prompts, rules, or reward models provide non-adaptive guidance throughout training. Such static signals are often sufficient to enforce output formats, but fail to shape the underlying reasoning process, leading to brittle generalization and performance saturation in complex decision-making tasks. We propose Evo-PI, a principle-centric learning framework that treats reasoning principles as explicit, language-based supervision signals that can be generated, evaluated, and iteratively evolved. Instead of relying on fixed rewards, Evo-PI enables a co-evolutionary loop in which principles guide model reasoning, while model behaviors in turn refine the principles that supervise them. This dynamic alignment mechanism allows supervision to progressively adapt to the model's reasoning deficiencies. We instantiate Evo-PI in medical visual question answering as a high-stakes testbed requiring structured visual-textual reasoning. Across eight benchmarks and multiple model backbones, Evo-PI consistently improves reasoning accuracy, achieving gains of up to 24.6%. Our results suggest that evolving principle-guided supervision offers a scalable and general paradigm for training expert-aligned reasoning in MLLMs. Code is available at https://github.com/zhengxianda/Evo_PI.
2606.31800.paper_summary_zh: 儘管最近取得了一些進展，大型多模態語言模型（MLLMs）的推理能力仍然受到靜態監督的根本限制，其中固定的提示、規則或獎勵模型在訓練過程中提供非自適應的指導。這種靜態信號通常足以強制執行輸出格式，但無法塑造基礎的推理過程，導致在複雜決策任務中的脆弱泛化和性能飽和。我們提出了Evo-PI，一種以原則為中心的學習框架，將推理原則視為明確的、基於語言的監督信號，這些信號可以被生成、評估並迭代演化。Evo-PI不依賴於固定的獎勵，而是使得原則引導模型推理的共同演化循環成為可能，同時模型行為反過來又精煉了監督它們的原則。這種動態對齊機制使得監督能夠逐步適應模型的推理缺陷。我們在醫療視覺問答中實現了Evo-PI，這是一個需要結構化視覺-文本推理的高風險測試平台。在八個基準和多個模型骨幹中，Evo-PI持續提高推理準確性，獲得高達24.6%的增益。我們的結果表明，演化的原則引導監督提供了一種可擴展且通用的範式，用於訓練與專家對齊的MLLM推理。代碼可在 https://github.com/zhengxianda/Evo_PI 獲得。
2606.31800.author: Xianda Zheng et.al.
2606.31800.authors: Xianda Zheng, Huan Gao, Meng-Fen Chiang, Michael Witbrock, Kaiqi Zhao, Shangyang Li
